# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 104

This is turn 104. Turns 1 through 103 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 104.

## Turn 104 — faithful-image descent repairs the local determinant paragraph

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In the proof, `40491355905` denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); the integer is only its verifier residue. 

Turn 103 contains one genuine notational—and potentially logical—ambiguity. It fixes

\[
H=G/L,
\]

but later says that “faithful-image projectivity permits an \(H\)-split embedding.” Projectivity need not hold over this full \(H\): an extra unramified \(2\)-power subgroup may act trivially on \(V\). The history already isolated exactly this phenomenon and repaired it by passing to the faithful image. 

The correction is uniform.

### Faithful-image descent lemma

Let \(V\) be the nontrivial simple tame module exposed in the exact-image induction, and set

\[
H_V=\operatorname{im}\!\left(H\longrightarrow \operatorname{GL}(V)\right),
\qquad
\pi_V:H\twoheadrightarrow H_V.
\]

Let \(\alpha_V=\pi_V\alpha\).

Then the pure determinant calculation on both sources depends only on the faithful frame \(\alpha_V\), not on the full frame \(\alpha\).

On the candidate side, the coefficient complex and determinant form use only the action matrices

\[
S=\alpha_V(\sigma),\qquad T=\alpha_V(\tau),\qquad
U=S^{\omega _2}.
\]

In particular,

\[
Q_A(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+B(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0,
\end{cases}
\]

is unchanged after replacing \(H\) by \(H_V\).

On the local side, let \(K_V/\mathbb Q_2\) be the tame extension fixed by the kernel of the representation on \(V\). Then

\[
\operatorname{Gal}(K_V/\mathbb Q_2)=H_V
\]

and the intrinsic connecting quadratic map is computed from

\[
H^1(\mathbb Q_2,V)
 \cong
\operatorname{Hom}_{H_V}
 \!\left(V^\vee,K_V^\times/K_V^{\times2}\right).
\]

If \(V\) is ramified, it is projective over its **faithful** tame image \(H_V\). Thus an \(H_V\)-split embedding into a free module exists, and the Shapiro/corestriction deep-unit argument applies exactly as in Turn 73. 

If \(V\) is unramified, its faithful image is cyclic of odd order, and the same norm–trace minus-form calculation applies.

The extra kernel

\[
\ker(H\to H_V)
\]

may destroy projectivity over the full \(H\), but it cannot alter the pure determinant quadratic form: it acts trivially on \(V\), \(V^\vee\), and \(\operatorname{Sym}^2(V^\vee)\). Any information involving that kernel belongs to the inflated or filtration-one components, which Turn 103 separates before invoking the pure determinant calculation.

Therefore the full tame frame must remain fixed for the global exact-image induction, but the determinant subroutine must descend to \(H_V\).

The Turn-103 paragraph should consequently read:

> Let \(H_V\) be the faithful tame image on \(V\), and let \(K_V/\mathbb Q_2\) be its tame splitting field. Faithful-image projectivity supplies an \(H_V\)-split embedding into a free module. After separating the pure determinant class from its inflated and filtration-one components, Shapiro over \(K_V/\mathbb Q_2\) turns each quadratic orbit monomial into a corestriction of scalar cup products or squares.

With this replacement, the apparent conflict with the earlier nonfaithful \(C_3\rtimes C_4\) example disappears, and equations (103.15)–(103.16) remain valid uniformly for every fixed tame frame. 

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved a faithful-image descent lemma for the determinant interface. The candidate and local pure determinant quadratic forms depend only on \(H_V=\operatorname{im}(H\to\operatorname{GL}(V))\); projectivity and the Shapiro/deep-unit argument must be invoked over \(H_V\), not necessarily over the full fixed tame quotient \(H=G/L\). This repairs the ambiguous Turn-103 sentence without changing the candidate or the exact-image induction.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The consolidated proof still requires independent line-by-line manuscript review, but this faithful-versus-nonfaithful tame-image issue is now removed.

**NEXT**: Insert the faithful-image descent lemma into the standalone version of the local determinant proposition, keeping the full tame frame only in the outer exact-image induction, and then audit the adjacent simple-head determinant reduction for the same quotient-label discipline.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
